steps.title: Identify what each term represents
steps.content: In the linear function f(x) = 6x + 9, the number 6 is the slope (how many feet the tree grows each year) and the number 9 is the constant term (the height when x = 0, at the moment of first measurement).

steps.title: Find the height at the initial measurement
steps.content: Since x represents years after the first measurement, when x = 0 we are at the moment of first measurement. Substituting into the function: $f(0) = 6(0) + 9 = 9$ feet. This is the tree's estimated height when it was first recorded. That is the correct answer.
